brief: Opportunities are heavily concentrated in Brooklyn R7A parcels, where market-tier projects pairing a 485-x tax exemption with Section 8 VPS rents generate the highest scores (100.0) and cash-on-cash returns up to 13.7%. Significant clusters exist around Gerry Street, Bartlett Street, and St Marks Avenue, though all top-performing deals are city-owned properties. Conversely, program-tier Bronx sites under an ELLA/LIHTC stack show estimated land costs exceeding walk-away limits, relying heavily on developer fees and subsidy wins.

targets.play: Develop a 12-unit residential building utilizing a 485-x tax exemption paired with Section 8 VPS rents to achieve a 13.7% cash-on-cash return.
targets.negotiation: Estimated land cost of $591,738 provides $996,310 in headroom against the $1,588,048 walk-away land threshold.
targets.risk: Requires navigating the public HPD/EDC disposition RFP process for city-owned land alongside standard zoning verification.

targets.negotiation: Estimated land cost of $569,141 leaves $956,661 in headroom relative to the $1,525,802 walk-away limit.
targets.risk: Subject to city-owned site HPD disposition procedures and detailed physical site condition diligence.

targets.play: Deliver a 14-unit development within a high-density Williamsburg cluster using a 485-x tax exemption and Section 8 VPS overlay.
targets.negotiation: Estimated land cost of $690,612 allows for $1,070,155 in negotiating headroom below the $1,760,767 walk-away land cap.
targets.risk: City-owned status requires winning an HPD disposition RFP, alongside verifying R7A bulk and site conditions.

targets.play: Execute a 14-unit Queens development yielding an 11.2% cash-on-cash return through a 485-x exemption and Section 8 VPS rent structure.
targets.negotiation: Estimated land cost of $622,014 offers $909,591 in headroom under the $1,531,605 walk-away limit.

process_note: Next steps are to verify zoning and site conditions for top Brooklyn targets while tracking HPD/EDC disposition schedules for upcoming municipal RFPs.
